area: MSC 37P05, 37P30, 37P50, 11G50 — arithmetic dynamics of correspondences: canonical heights, dynamical degrees, Berkovich potential theory, dynamical Mordell-Lang
hole_justification: arXiv full-text API searches (all July 2026): "Collatz"+"canonical height" = 0 hits; "Collatz"+"Berkovich" = 0; "Collatz"+"dynamical degree" = 0; "Collatz"+"Mordell-Lang" = 0; "Collatz"+"solenoid" = 0; "Collatz"+("adelic"|"adeles"|"product formula") = 0; "Collatz"+("measure rigidity"|"Rudolph"|"Hecke correspondence") = 0. Web searches ("Collatz correspondence height", "arithmetic dynamics Collatz", "Collatz Berkovich equidistribution potential theory", Silverman+Collatz, MathOverflow) found no treatment of 3x+1 via correspondence heights/dynamical degrees/Berkovich machinery. The bridge itself is essentially virgin; I grade "fringe" because three adjacent bodies of work exist and must be acknowledged: (1) Siegel's (p,q)-adic "non-archimedean spectral theory" series (arXiv:2007.15936, published in p-Adic Numbers Ultrametric Anal. Appl. 2024) — genuinely non-archimedean (Z_2→Z_3 "Numen" function, Correspondence Principle for periodic points, divergence criterion) but Fourier/Wiener-tauberian, no heights, no potential theory, no correspondence formalism; (2) Applegate–Lagarias "The 3x+1 semigroup" (J. Number Theory 2006, arXiv:math/0411140) + Caraiani's follow-up — the multivalued/semigroup viewpoint exists but is treated by multiplicative-semigroup combinatorics, not dynamical heights; (3) Applegate–Lagarias density bounds I–II and Krasikov–Lagarias inequalities — inverse-tree counting mod 3^k, which is de facto the transfer operator at the place 3 in disguise, never phrased potential-theoretically. Lagarias 1985 (2-adic shift conjugacy) and Tao's 2011 blog "The Collatz conjecture, Littlewood-Offord theory, and powers of 2 and 3" are the nearest ×2×3-adjacent items. Nobody has written down the Collatz correspondence with Ingram/Autissier heights, Dinh-Sibony degrees, or Baker-Rumely/Favre-Rivera-Letelier equidistribution.
bridge: Collatz (T-normalization) is the reducible algebraic correspondence C: (2y−x)(2y−3x−1)=0 in A^1×A^1 over Q, i.e. C = Γ_{g0} ∪ Γ_{g1} with g0(x)=x/2, g1(x)=(3x+1)/2, closed up in P^1×P^1: bidegree (d1,d2)=(2,2), both projections finite surjective — exactly Ingram's setting. Ingram's path space P (paths in the directed graph with shift σ) is literally Lagarias's Z_2: the parity vector identifies P with Z_2 and σ with T, so the abstract correspondence formalism reproduces the 2-adic conjugacy. Integer Collatz orbits = Q-rational paths selected by congruence (branch g1 iff x odd). What corresponds to what: forward images ↔ {x/2, (3x+1)/2}; backward correspondence ↔ the semigroup ⟨y↦2y, y↦(2y−1)/3⟩ whose orbit of 1 is the Collatz tree; Weil height h = log|·| on orbits ↔ the drift functional; the local heights at v=2,3,∞ ↔ the three terms of the product-formula identity log x_k − log x_0 = (log 3)·#odd steps − (log 2)·k + O(1). Structural facts I verified/computed: (a) C is NOT polarized: both branches are Möbius graphs, so y*L ≡ x*L on C, forcing α=1, while Ingram (Thm 1.1) and Autissier both require α>1 — the failure of polarization IS the drift, and no canonical height exists; (b) all dynamical degrees of components are 1; d_j(C^n)=2^n but every component of C^n has bidegree (1,1) and every length-n path curve is rational (genus 0 forever), so Ingram's genus-growth/Faltings trick (his Example 1.4) fails structurally — the entropy log 2 of the correspondence is purely combinatorial (the 2-shift), invisible to heights; (c) Kawaguchi-Silverman is trivially satisfied (arithmetic degree 1 = dynamical degree 1 along every path) with zero content; (d) the potential-theoretic dichotomy "ĥ=0 ⇔ bounded height ⇔ preperiodic" is unusable and would be circular: for α=1, "does h(T^k n) stay bounded" IS the conjecture; (e) over C the correspondence is weakly modular in the Dinh–Kaufmann–Wu sense — δ_∞ at the common fixed point ∞ of both branches is a (degenerate) modular measure — so even the balanced-case (d1=d2) equidistribution theorem does not apply; the degenerate modular measure sits exactly at the divergence locus.
strongest_adjacent_theorems: Ingram, 'Canonical heights for correspondences' (arXiv:1411.1041): for C⊆X×X with finite surjective projections, polarized by ample L with y*L ~ α·x*L, α>1, there is ĥ on the path space with ĥ∘σ = α·ĥ and ĥ = h∘π + O(1); Cor 1.3: points carrying a repetitive path form a set of bounded height (finitely many rational ones). Requires α>1 — fails for Collatz (α=1).

Autissier, 'Dynamique des correspondances algébriques et hauteurs', IMRN 2004 no. 69, 3723–3739: canonical heights on 1-cycles for algebraic correspondences on curves with nonzero 'charge' h_0, plus equidistribution of small points; again empty when the charge/polarization degenerates (Collatz has h_0 = 0 in his normalization).

Dinh–Kaufmann–Wu, 'Dynamics of holomorphic correspondences on Riemann surfaces', Int. J. Math. 31 (2020) 2050036 (arXiv:1808.10130): for a BALANCED correspondence (d1=d2=d≥2) that is non-weakly-modular, two canonical measures μ± exist with f*μ+=dμ+ and d^{-n}(f^n)*α → c_α μ+ exponentially fast; preimages of every point equidistribute if no critical value of f is periodic. The exact balanced case Collatz lives in — but Collatz is weakly modular via δ_∞, so the theorem's necessary hypothesis fails, precisely at the divergence locus.

Clozel–Ullmo, 'Équidistribution des points de Hecke' (in Contributions to Automorphic Forms, 2004): balanced modular correspondences (Hecke) DO equidistribute toward the invariant measure — the model of what a 'modular' balanced correspondence with good equidistribution looks like.

Baker–Rumely (Ann. Inst. Fourier 56, 2006), Favre–Rivera-Letelier (Math. Ann. 335, 2006), Chambert-Loir (Crelle 595, 2006): equidistribution of points of small canonical height on the Berkovich projective line; the bounded-height/preperiodic dichotomy for degree ≥ 2 rational maps. The machinery the push must adapt — it needs degree ≥ 2, which Collatz branches never have.

Truong, 'Relative dynamical degrees of correspondences over fields of arbitrary characteristic', J. reine angew. Math. 758 (2020); Dinh–Sibony, Comment. Math. Helv. 81 (2006): existence of dynamical degrees and equidistribution for meromorphic transforms/correspondences with dominant topological degree — for Collatz all algebraic degrees are 1 and topological degree 2 is pure branching (the 2-shift).

Rudolph–Johnson measure rigidity (Ergodic Theory Dynam. Systems 1990) and Bourgain–Lindenstrauss–Michel–Venkatesh, 'Some effective results for ×a×b' (ETDS 29, 2009): positive-entropy joint ×2,×3-invariant measures are Lebesgue, with effective versions — the deep rigidity adjacent to the (2,3)-adic skew structure of Collatz; no analog is known for the affine solvable semigroup ⟨x/2, (3x+1)/2⟩.

Xie, 'Around the dynamical Mordell-Lang conjecture' (arXiv:2307.05885): DML for coherent backward orbits — but on A^1 subvarieties are points, so DML is contentless for Collatz; the Collatz tree = coherent backward orbit of 1 needs a density statement, not an intersection statement.
the_push: First-order height machinery provably sees only drift: no polarization (α=1), all dynamical degrees 1, arithmetic degree 1, every path curve rational — the correspondence formalism explains WHY heights fail (the entropy log 2 is the 2-adic shift, i.e. combinatorial, and Faltings/genus obstructions never activate), which is worth stating but is a wall, not a tool. The genuinely unmined second-order structure is at the places 2 and 3 jointly. At v=3: g0(y)=y/2 is an isometry of Z_3 while g1(y)=(3y+1)/2 contracts by |3|_3 = 1/3, so the forward correspondence is contracting-on-average on the Berkovich unit disc over C_3, and for each odd-step frequency θ there is a unique stationary self-affine measure ν_θ on Z_3 (a 3-adic Bernoulli-convolution analog; NOT Haar — its self-similarity ν_θ = (1−θ)(g0)*ν_θ + θ(g1)*ν_θ is computable exactly mod 3^k by finite transfer matrices). The backward correspondence is expanding at 3, so backward-tree equidistribution toward ν with an explicit spectral gap is provable — this is the Berkovich-potential-theory packaging of exactly what Applegate–Lagarias/Krasikov tree-counting mod 3^k does by hand. The sharpest formulation of 'Collatz as equidistribution of an orbit measure': the skew product S(x,y) = (Tx, g_{parity(x)}(y)) on Z_2 × Z_3 is continuous (parity is clopen), has a unique invariant measure projecting to Haar_2, namely μ = Haar_2 ⋉ ν_{1/2}; a divergent integer orbit, embedded diagonally, must generate (subsequentially) exceptional S-invariant measures whose Z_2-marginal has 1-density θ ≥ log2/log3 ≈ 0.6309 and whose Z_3-marginal is the exceptional ν_θ, while the product formula pins archimedean growth to (3^θ/2) per step. So 'bounded height ⇔ preperiodic' is replaced by: divergence ⇔ anomalous parity entropy at 2 + anomalous local equidistribution at 3, coupled adelically. This lands the problem in Furstenberg ×2×3 rigidity territory (Rudolph–Johnson, BLMV) for an affine solvable skew action — where no rigidity theorem currently exists, and Bernoulli(θ) exceptional measures cannot yet be excluded (consistent with the known ones-ratio bound being exactly log2/log3, and with Tao 2019 stopping at log-density). Bonus structural observation: the archimedean random model X' = X/2 or (3X+1)/2 is a Kesten affine recursion with E[log A] < 0 but E[A] = (1/2)(1/2+3/2) = 1 exactly — tail index κ=1 (Cauchy tail, infinite mean) — the borderline that heuristically explains why log-density is the natural achievable density. Honest verdict: the bridge yields (i) a precise impossibility statement for first-order height methods, (ii) a clean, new-in-the-literature adelic/skew-product formulation with provable place-3 equidistribution and explicit gaps, (iii) a well-posed rigidity question to hand to homogeneous-dynamics people; it does not yield a visible route to the full conjecture.
first_move: Prove the uniform-in-level spectral gap at the place 3 ('Hölder continuity of the Collatz Green function on the Berkovich disc over C_3'). Concretely: (1) Computation (32 cores, a day): build the sparse transfer matrix W_k of the backward correspondence y↦2y (always), y↦(2y−1)/3 (iff y ≡ 2 mod 3) on Z/3^k for k ≤ 12 (3^12 ≈ 5.3e5 states), with generation weights matching the Applegate–Lagarias tree normalization; compute stationary vectors (= ν_θ mod 3^k for θ = branching frequency) and subdominant eigenvalues λ_2(k); conjecture and then prove λ_2(k) ≤ λ < 1 uniformly in k (finite check + a contraction/coupling argument on 3-adic digits — the coupling is level-1: the map y↦(2y−1)/3 forgets one ternary digit; this is Lean-formalizable since the core is finite linear algebra plus an induction on digits). (2) Lemma to attempt: 'The backward Collatz tree equidistributes in Z_3 with exponential error: for every ball B ⊆ Z_3 of radius 3^{-k}, the fraction of depth-n tree vertices in B is ν(B) + O(λ^n) uniformly in k ≤ cn.' (3) Payoff test: recast the Krasikov–Lagarias inequality system as eigenvalue/LP bounds on W_k restricted to the tree constraints and check on the machines whether the potential-theoretic normalization improves the known density exponent x^0.84 for the set of n ≤ x reaching 1 — that comparison (improve/recover/fail) is decisive for whether the bridge earns a paper beyond the framework. In parallel, run the second-order numerical test of the framework's prediction: empirical distribution of odd iterates mod 3^k along orbits of random n ~ 2^60 should match ν_{1/2} mod 3^k (computable exactly from W_k), with discrepancy decaying at the predicted rate.
payoff_odds: Realistic best case (~10-15%): the uniform place-3 spectral gap + adelic skew-product framework yields a genuine improvement or clean conceptual re-derivation of the Applegate–Lagarias/Krasikov density exponent (x^0.84), plus a publishable 'why heights cannot see Collatz' impossibility theorem (no polarization, degree-1 components, rational path curves) and a precisely posed measure-rigidity question for the affine (2,3)-skew action — a solid paper bridging arithmetic dynamics and Collatz, and a plausible new attack surface for homogeneous-dynamics experts. Likely case (~60%): the transfer-operator packaging exactly reproduces known tree-counting bounds with no exponent improvement; deliverables shrink to the framework paper, the Kesten-tail and 3-adic-equidistribution observations, and negative/structural results. Failure mode (~25%): the place-3 gap is uniform but the coupling to the 2-adic shift adds nothing beyond drift, in which case the honest conclusion is that this bridge terminates at the same Bernoulli(θ)-exceptional-measure wall as Tao 2019 and the ones-ratio bounds. Probability this route resolves Collatz: negligible. The area is a real hole, but it is a hole because the first-order machinery provably degenerates — the mining value is in the second-order (local, adelic, rigidity) layer, and that layer is genuinely unexplored in print.

prior_art.ref: Ingram, P., 'Canonical heights for correspondences', arXiv:1411.1041 (2014/2017)
prior_art.what: Builds Call–Silverman canonical heights on the path space of a polarized correspondence (y*L ~ α·x*L, α>1); bounded height for repetitive paths. Never mentions Collatz; his α>1 hypothesis fails for the Collatz correspondence (α=1), which is the precise obstruction.

prior_art.ref: Autissier, P., 'Dynamique des correspondances algébriques et hauteurs', IMRN 2004:69, 3723-3739
prior_art.what: Canonical heights and equidistribution of small points for correspondences on curves with nonzero charge; degenerates for charge-0 (balanced affine) correspondences like Collatz. No Collatz mention.

prior_art.ref: Dinh, T.-C., Kaufmann, L., Wu, H., 'Dynamics of holomorphic correspondences on Riemann surfaces', Int. J. Math. 31 (2020)
prior_art.what: The balanced case d1=d2: canonical measures and exponential equidistribution under non-weak-modularity. Collatz is weakly modular via the common fixed point at infinity (δ_∞), so the theorem inapplicable — the degeneracy sits exactly at the divergence locus. No Collatz mention.

prior_art.ref: Siegel, M.C., 'The Collatz Conjecture & Non-Archimedean Spectral Theory, Part I', arXiv:2007.15936; published in p-Adic Numbers Ultrametric Anal. Appl. 16 (2024)
prior_art.what: Nearest non-archimedean prior art: (2,q)-adic 'Numen' χ_q: Z_2→Z_q with a Correspondence Principle characterizing periodic points and a divergence criterion; Fourier/Wiener-tauberian methods. NOT done: heights, Berkovich potential theory, correspondence/dynamical-degree formalism, equidistribution at 3.

prior_art.ref: Applegate, D., Lagarias, J.C., 'The 3x+1 semigroup', J. Number Theory 117 (2006), arXiv:math/0411140; Caraiani, 'Multiplicative semigroups related to the 3x+1 problem', Adv. Appl. Math. (2010)
prior_art.what: The multivalued/semigroup viewpoint on record: proves Farkas's weak 3x+1 conjecture (the 3x+1 semigroup contains all positive integers) by multiplicative-semigroup combinatorics. NOT done: dynamical heights, invariant measures, spectral gaps.

prior_art.ref: Applegate–Lagarias, 'Density bounds for the 3x+1 problem I, II' (Math. Comp. 64, 1995); Krasikov–Lagarias, 'Bounds for the 3x+1 problem using difference inequalities' (Acta Arith. 109, 2003)
prior_art.what: Inverse-tree counting via congruence classes mod 3^k giving density exponent x^0.84 — de facto the place-3 transfer operator, never phrased as Berkovich potential theory or equidistribution; the benchmark the first move must beat or cleanly recover.

prior_art.ref: Tao, T., blog 'The Collatz conjecture, Littlewood-Offord theory, and powers of 2 and 3' (2011); Tao, 'Almost all orbits of the Collatz map attain almost bounded values' (Forum Math. Pi 2022, arXiv:1909.03562)
prior_art.what: The ×2×3/Z[1/6] adjacency (Diophantine, via Baker-type bounds on 2^a 3^b) and the state-of-the-art log-density result; neither uses correspondence heights or local potential theory at 3.

prior_art.ref: Bullett–Lomonaco (+Penrose), matings of quadratic maps with the modular group; 'Equidistribution for matings...' arXiv:2202.06910
prior_art.what: The developed dynamical theory of (2,2)-correspondences on P^1 — the same bidegree class as Collatz — showing balanced correspondences can carry deep structure; all holomorphic/archimedean, nothing arithmetic or Collatz-related.
